texto: And look at all I've accumulated---a house! piles of clothing! two children! an ex-husband! books! boxes of letters! dishes! tiny shampoos from fancy hotels! vases! canned goods! jewelry! computers! acres of old journals! couches! bedsteads! toys galore! stuffed animals! and heaps of memories like wet rags, bunches of them, hanging off me, weighing me down.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Martha Tod Dudman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “And look at all I've accumulated---a house! piles of clothing! two children! an ex-husband! books…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, vida, memoir pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de memoir e memories.
